best_edges.action: protect and use as baseline expansion engine
best_edges.evidence: pullback_long is the largest durable contributor: 31 trades, $2404.6 PnL, 251.35 PnL/exposure hour, 12.0 minute median hold, no slow trades. Prior pullback structure tightening from run_012 to run_015 improved score materially, but later widening TP/VWAP in run_018 caused a severe drawdown/MAE failure, so expansion must be narrow.

best_edges.action: protect; only test asymmetric upside when exposure does not lengthen
best_edges.evidence: charm_wall_breakaway_long produced $985.6 from only 5 trades with 234.36 PnL/exposure hour and median MFE 146.2. The exit-compression test hurt objective by -143.6 score and -$146.7 net profit, showing the profile's payoff comes from preserving larger targets rather than compressing exits.

extended_vwap_winners produced 18 trades and $674.9 with median MAE only -14.875 and median hold 1.725 minutes, even though the worst tail is also extended. This means blunt ES-VWAP distance caps would damage a real fast-profit edge.

best_edges.action: keep active but do not tighten with prior failed gates
best_edges.evidence: breakout_long_1dte contributed 20 trades, $1298.7 PnL, no slow trades, and 167.94 PnL/exposure hour. Prior 1DTE local quality gates reduced score and net profit, so the 1DTE edge is real but sensitive to direct structure/slope tightening.

best_edges.action: protect as clean continuation sleeve; expand only with hold-time guardrails
best_edges.evidence: squeeze_long contributed $721 from 10 trades, no slow trades, worst MAE -208.25, median MFE 77.8. Prior squeeze TP expansion was flat in later tests, suggesting current realized exits may already be binding or unchanged, but the profile remains a clean supplemental edge.

broken_edges.action: freeze broad risk pruning; only use surgical or path-aware research
broken_edges.evidence: All trades are winners, but worst MAE remains -527 and bad-MAE filters frequently block too much protected PnL. Example: setup_quality_score >= 62 would retain only 43% PnL; ADX slope >= 0 would retain only 58.9% PnL and block 24 protected good trades.
broken_edges.problem: Broad entry-quality filters do not separate recoverable heat from bad heat.

broken_edges.evidence: Two prior 1DTE tightening tests hurt objective: run_005 to run_008 lost -594 score and -$558.5 net profit; run_004 to run_007 lost -355.7 score and -$317.1 net profit.
broken_edges.problem: Simple structure/slope/take-profit tightening degrades the 1DTE edge.

broken_edges.action: freeze no-0DTE fallback optimization unless isolated as a tiny-sample holdout
broken_edges.evidence: STALE_0DTE contributed only 5 trades and $145.8; no-0DTE breakout holdout improved worst MAE but lost -$157.2 and -120 score. MISSING_0DTE routed profiles had no trades.
broken_edges.problem: Fallback samples are too small to justify leader-level profit-expansion tuning.

broken_edges.evidence: Charm-wall exit compression increased trade count but reduced objective and net profit, confirming that reducing the target/multiplier sacrifices the profile's main payoff.
broken_edges.problem: Exit compression weakens a convex high-MFE profile.

broken_edges.action: treat dormant enabled=false profiles as activation tests only, not optimization targets
broken_edges.evidence: breakout_long, wall_reclaim_long, wall_breakout_retest_long, late_day_pin_fade, negative_gamma_momentum_long, and trend_day_pullback_long produced zero trades while disabled. They have no observed edge in this leader context.
broken_edges.problem: Dead or unreachable active-router profiles should not receive parameter tuning before activation evidence exists.

market_regime_read: The sample is a clean long-only momentum/pullback tape with 100% realized win rate, no slow >=12h trades, low portfolio drawdown, and profits concentrated in VALID_0DTE pullback/charm/squeeze plus a meaningful VALID_1DTE_ONLY breakout fallback. The main risk is hidden heat, not realized losses: average MAE near -99, worst MAE -527, and only 9.4% of trades had MFE before MAE. Profit expansion should therefore seek higher PnL per exposure hour from already proven sleeves while preserving fast-winner fingerprints and rejecting any experiment that lengthens holds or worsens MAE.

new_strategy_ideas.edge: Separate high-extension charm-pin recoveries from high-extension traps using early path response rather than static entry state.
new_strategy_ideas.failure_mode: A config-only threshold on VWAP extension or pullback-from-high will block clean extended-VWAP winners with median MAE around -15.
new_strategy_ideas.name: Charm Pin Early-Progress Tail Classifier
new_strategy_ideas.required_data: Intratrade first 1-15 minute MFE/MAE, positive-before-MAE flag, TP-touch timing, profile, ES-VWAP distance, VWAP acceleration, pullback-from-high, RSI slope.
new_strategy_ideas.trigger: charm_pin_pullback_long entries with high ES-VWAP extension and warning points that fail to print early positive progress.
new_strategy_ideas.validation_metric: Reduce charm_pin_pullback_long worst MAE from -527 toward <= -403 while retaining >=90% charm-pin PnL and >=95% protected fast-winner count.

new_strategy_ideas.edge: Route VALID_1DTE_ONLY pullback/reclaim states separately instead of tightening breakout_long_1dte.
new_strategy_ideas.failure_mode: Router ordering or trigger overlap may cannibalize profitable breakout_long_1dte flow.
new_strategy_ideas.name: 1DTE Pullback Companion
new_strategy_ideas.required_data: 1DTE option map state, ES reclaim distance, pullback/reclaim flags, VWAP slope, structure score, forward DEX ratio, existing 1DTE trade attribution.
new_strategy_ideas.trigger: 0DTE unavailable, 1DTE map valid, and ES state resembles pullback/reclaim rather than breakout continuation.
new_strategy_ideas.validation_metric: Combined 1DTE PnL >= current $1298.7, 1DTE average MAE improves versus -123.1, and total net profit remains >=98% of leader.

new_strategy_ideas.edge: Use an inactive charm/pin avoidance sleeve as a router-level split for high pin-risk states currently entering charm_pin_pullback_long or charm_wall_breakaway_long.
new_strategy_ideas.failure_mode: Dormant profile may be unreachable or may steal clean fast charm winners instead of isolating tails.
new_strategy_ideas.name: Charm Wall Pin Avoid Activation Holdout
new_strategy_ideas.required_data: Router activation for inactive charm_wall_pin_avoid_long or late_day_pin_avoid_long, standalone profile attribution, protected extended-VWAP winner overlap.
new_strategy_ideas.trigger: VALID_0DTE high charm-wall or pin-risk state currently routed into charm_pin_pullback_long or charm_wall_breakaway_long.
new_strategy_ideas.validation_metric: Activated profile has positive standalone PnL or reduces combined charm/pin worst MAE, total net profit >=98% of leader, and fast-winner count not materially reduced.

new_strategy_ideas.edge: Identify pullback trades that can accept wider profit capture without recreating the run_018 tail.
new_strategy_ideas.failure_mode: Static TP/VWAP widening previously created a severe slow-heat event and reduced score.
new_strategy_ideas.name: Pullback MFE Realization Split
new_strategy_ideas.required_data: Pullback path MFE timing, first-profit timing, ES structure/reclaim state, selected TP distance, hold-time distribution.
new_strategy_ideas.trigger: pullback_long entries with strong early MFE and clean reclaim behavior where current TP exits before available continuation.
new_strategy_ideas.validation_metric: pullback_long PnL/exposure improves above 275 with total worst MAE not worse than -527 and no slow >=12h trades.

next_experiments.config_bias: Favor micro-expansion of pullback_long only in states resembling current profitable pullbacks: moderate ES-VWAP distance, acceptable structure/reclaim, and no increase in maximum allowed extension. Do not repeat the run_018 wide-VWAP/TP expansion.
next_experiments.goal: Increase total net profit and PnL/exposure without reintroducing slow heat.
next_experiments.hypothesis: The pullback engine can add PnL if the optimizer expands only within current high-efficiency reclaim states, not by admitting wider extension.
next_experiments.name: Pullback Conservative Throughput Re-Expansion
next_experiments.primary_metric: Risk-adjusted score with net profit > leader and pnl_per_exposure_hour >=197 while worst MAE no worse than -527 and slow_12h_trades = 0.
next_experiments.risk: Even small looseness in pullback admission can admit the heat pattern seen in run_018.

next_experiments.config_bias: Keep charm_wall_breakaway targets large; test only small admission-quality changes around confirmed breakaway context, not lower exits. Preserve its convex payoff.
next_experiments.goal: Add one or two high-quality charm-wall breakaway trades or improve profile PnL without reducing payoff size.
next_experiments.hypothesis: The profile is under-sampled but efficient; expansion should come from slightly broader confirmed entries, not compressed exits.
next_experiments.name: Charm Wall Breakaway Admission Probe
next_experiments.primary_metric: Profile PnL/exposure >=225, total net profit >= leader, completed trades not down, worst MAE no worse than -527.
next_experiments.risk: Five-trade sample is fragile; one lost winner can dominate the result.

next_experiments.config_bias: Test a surgical nudge to the already-enabled extended-VWAP rejection family only around near-miss thresholds, while explicitly protecting extended-VWAP fast winners. Use this as a risk-adjusted profit-expansion guard, not a broad pruning filter.
next_experiments.goal: Improve risk-adjusted score by removing or resizing the worst delayed-profit tails with minimal PnL loss.
next_experiments.hypothesis: The worst charm-pin tail missed the existing veto by a narrow VWAP-distance boundary, so a tiny threshold nudge may reduce MAE without damaging the fast extension edge.
next_experiments.name: Extended VWAP Near-Miss Micro Nudge
next_experiments.primary_metric: Worst MAE improves toward <= -403 or average MAE improves, with total PnL retained >=98%, fast_under_15m trades down <=2, and protected extended-VWAP winners mostly retained.
next_experiments.risk: The same signature overlaps fast extended-VWAP winners; over-nudging will reduce throughput and profit.

next_experiments.config_bias: Do not tighten breakout_long_1dte directly. Instead test a router/profile split for pullback_long_1dte as an isolated activation holdout, with breakout_long_1dte preservation as a hard success criterion.
next_experiments.goal: Improve 1DTE risk-adjusted efficiency without damaging the existing 1DTE fallback edge.
next_experiments.hypothesis: VALID_1DTE_ONLY has real PnL but high average MAE; a companion pullback/reclaim sleeve may separate cleaner 1DTE states better than stricter breakout filters.
next_experiments.name: 1DTE Pullback Companion Activation
next_experiments.primary_metric: Combined 1DTE PnL >= $1298.7, 1DTE average MAE improves versus -123.1, total net profit >=98% of leader, and breakout_long_1dte PnL not materially cannibalized.
next_experiments.risk: The dormant companion may be unreachable or router order may divert profitable breakout trades.

next_experiments.config_bias: Keep squeeze_long enabled and probe only one small profit-expansion dimension at a time, prioritizing states with strong ES-VWAP slope and option-map confirmation. Avoid broader TP changes that produced flat results unless paired with a cleaner entry subset.
next_experiments.goal: Extract more from clean squeeze continuation without longer holds.
next_experiments.hypothesis: Squeeze has low tail risk and strong MFE; expansion may work only when conditioned on stronger continuation quality, not through unconditional target increases.
next_experiments.name: Squeeze Quality-Conditioned Upside Probe
next_experiments.primary_metric: squeeze_long PnL and average PnL improve, total net profit >=98% of leader, pnl_per_exposure_hour flat or better, worst MAE no worse than -527.
next_experiments.risk: Higher targets can simply extend exposure without improving realized exits, as prior squeeze TP probes were flat.

next_experiments.config_bias: Run a config-only dormant activation holdout for one pin-avoid concept only if the router replacement can isolate it cleanly; measure standalone attribution before any tuning.
next_experiments.goal: Discover whether existing dormant pin-avoid playbook logic can harvest or deflect high charm/pin-risk states.
next_experiments.hypothesis: Some charm-pin heat belongs in a separate avoid/reclaim sleeve rather than being filtered globally.
next_experiments.name: Dormant Charm Pin Avoid Activation Holdout
next_experiments.primary_metric: Activated profile standalone PnL positive or combined charm/pin worst MAE improves, total net profit >=98% of leader, protected fast-winner count retained.
next_experiments.risk: Activation may be unreachable or may cannibalize clean charm_pin_pullback_long and charm_wall_breakaway_long winners.

research_notes: The current leader family is already near a plateau: many top runs are identical or effectively tied, and upside TP extension candidates are empty. The best profit-expansion path is not more global looseness. It is selective: protect pullback_long, charm_wall_breakaway_long, squeeze_long, and breakout_long_1dte; avoid broad quality gates; use dormant/profile splits where direct tightening has failed. The strongest warning is overlap between bad-MAE tails and protected winners: static features alone are weak separators, while early path behavior is much cleaner but likely requires code.
risk_controls: Reject if worst MAE worsens beyond -527 or average MAE deteriorates materially without a clear net-profit gain.
Reject if total net profit falls below 98% of leader for any risk-reducer/profile-specific test.
For profit-expansion tests, require pnl_per_exposure_hour flat or better unless net profit gain is large and MAE unchanged.
Protect fast_under_15m winners; trade-count loss above 5-10% needs a major MAE or PnL/exposure improvement.
Do not repeat failed broad 1DTE structure/slope tightening or pullback wide-VWAP/TP expansion.
Treat no-0DTE fallback changes as isolated holdouts only due to tiny sample size.
